parameters.name: glslversion
parameters.label: GLSL Version
parameters.description: Pick what version of GLSL to compile the shader with.

parameters.name: predat
parameters.label: Preprocess Directives
parameters.description: Use this to place preprocessor directives at the start of your shader, such as #extension. This is required since these need to be the first lines in the shader, and TouchDesigner will be adding code

parameters.name: vdat
parameters.label: Vertex Shader
parameters.description: to the that holds the vertex shader code.

parameters.name: pdat
parameters.label: Pixel Shader
parameters.description: to the that holds the pixel shader code.

parameters.name: loaduniformnames
parameters.label: Load Uniform Names
parameters.description: This will read all the declared (and used) uniforms in the compiled GLSL shader, and fill in the various name fields for the uniform parameters.

parameters.name: clearuniformnames
parameters.label: Clear Uniform Names
parameters.description: This will clear all of the name fields for the uniform parameters.

parameters.name: gdat
parameters.label: Geometry Shader
parameters.description: to the that holds the geometry shader code.

parameters.name: inherit
parameters.label: Inherit Uniforms/Samplers from
parameters.description: This will inherit all of the Textures and Uniforms from the GLSL referenced in this parameter.

parameters.name: lightingspace
parameters.label: Lighting Space
parameters.description: Allows lighting space switch from the current default World Space to legacy Camera Space which was used for TouchDesigner 088.

parameters.name: simplexnoise
parameters.label: TDSimplexNoise()
parameters.description: Pick between Performance vs Quality Simplex noise. Performance mode picks the existing TDSimplexNoise(). Quality noise reduces certain artifacts and axis alignment issues with the existing simplex noi

parameters.name: attr
parameters.label: Attribute
parameters.description: A sequence of attributes that should be made accesible in the shader. In the past these would be manually declared inside the shader, but to add support for POP based workflows, they need to be setup

parameters.name: attr0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: The name to give the attribute.

parameters.name: attr0type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The type, both vector size as well as if it's a float, double (d), integer (i), or unsigned integer (u).

parameters.name: sampler
parameters.label: Sampler
parameters.description: Sequence of samplers for use in the shader

parameters.name: sampler0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: This is the sampler name that the GLSL program will use to sample from this . The samplers need to be declared at the same dimentions as the (sampler2D for a 2D , sampler3D for 3D ).

parameters.name: sampler0top
parameters.label: TOP
parameters.description: This is the that will be referenced by the above sampler name above it.

parameters.name: sampler0extendu
parameters.label: Extend U
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sampler0extendv
parameters.label: Extend V
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sampler0extendw
parameters.label: Extend W
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sampler0filter
parameters.label: Filter
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: sampler0anisotropy
parameters.label: Anisotropic Filter
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: vec
parameters.label: Vector
parameters.description: Sequence of vectors to be used as uniforms

parameters.name: vec0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: The name of the vector.

parameters.name: array
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: Sequence of arrays to be used as uniforms

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.label: Matrix
parameters.description: Sequence of matrices to be used as uniforms

parameters.name: matrix0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: The name of the uniform. The uniform should be declared as a mat4.

parameters.name: matrix0value
parameters.label: Matrix
parameters.description: The value to assign the matrix. For valid ways to specify this, see the article.

parameters.name: rel
parameters.label: Relative
parameters.description: A sequence of relative transforms. The transform is the relative transform one Object to another.

parameters.name: rel0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: The name of the uniform. The uniform should be declared as a mat4.

parameters.name: rel0from
parameters.label: Xform from
parameters.description: Transform from this COMPs world transform.

parameters.name: rel0to
parameters.label: To
parameters.description: Transform to this COMPs world transform.

parameters.name: const
parameters.label: Constant
parameters.description: Sequence of constant uniforms.

parameters.name: const0name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: The constant name, as declared in the shader.

parameters.name: const0value
parameters.label: Value
parameters.description: The value to give the constant.

parameters.name: dodeform
parameters.label: Deform
parameters.description: Enables deforms on this material.

parameters.name: deformdata
parameters.label: Get Bone Data:
parameters.description: Specifies where the deform bone data will be obtained.

parameters.name: targetsop
parameters.label: SOP with Capture Data
parameters.description: Specifies the that contains the deform capture attributes.

parameters.name: pcaptpath
parameters.label: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.description: Specifies the name of the pCaptPath attribute to use. When your geometry has been put through a , the attributes will be split into names like pCaptPath0, pCaptPath1. You can only render 1 bone group

parameters.name: pcaptdata
parameters.label: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.description: Much like pCaptPath Attrib.

parameters.name: skelrootpath
parameters.label: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.description: Specifies the path to the where the root of the skeleton is located.

parameters.name: mat
parameters.label: MAT
parameters.description: When obtaining deform data from a or a Deform In , this is where that is specified.

parameters.name: blending
parameters.label: Blending (Transparency)
parameters.description: This toggle enables and disables blending. However see the wiki article .

parameters.name: srcblend
parameters.label: Source Color *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Color).

parameters.name: destblend
parameters.label: Destination Color *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Color).

parameters.name: separatealphafunc
parameters.label: Separate Alpha Function
parameters.description: This toggle enables and disables separate blending options for the alpha values.

parameters.name: srcblenda
parameters.label: Source Alpha *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Alpha).

parameters.name: destblenda
parameters.label: Destination Alpha *
parameters.description: This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Alpha).

parameters.name: postmultalpha
parameters.label: Post-Mult Color by Alpha
parameters.description: Multiplies the color by alpha after all other operations have taken place.

parameters.name: depthtest
parameters.label: Depth Test
parameters.description: Enables and disables the Depth-Test. If the depth-test is disabled, depths values aren't written to the Depth-Buffer.

parameters.name: depthfunc
parameters.label: Depth Test Function
parameters.description: The depth value of the pixel being drawn is compared to the depth value currently in the depth-buffer using this function. If the test passes then the pixel is drawn to the Buffer. If the test fails t

parameters.name: depthwriting
parameters.label: Write Depth Values
parameters.description: If Write Depth Values is on, pixels that pass the depth-test will write their depth value to the Depth-Buffer. If this isn't on then no changes will be made to the Depth-Buffer, regardless of if the p

parameters.name: alphatest
parameters.label: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.description: This enables or disables the pixel alpha test.

parameters.name: alphafunc
parameters.label: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.description: This menu works in conjunction with the Alpha Threshold parameter below in determining which pixels to keep based on their alpha value.

parameters.name: alphathreshold
parameters.label: Alpha Threshold
parameters.description: This value is what the pixel's alpha is compared to to determine if the pixel should be drawn. Pixels with alpha greater than the Alpha Threshold will be drawn. Pixels with alpha less than or equal to

parameters.name: wireframe
parameters.label: Wire Frame
parameters.description: Enables and disables wire-frame rendering with the option of OpenGL Tesselated or Topology based wireframes.

parameters.name: wirewidth
parameters.label: Line Width
parameters.description: This value is the width that the wires will be. This value is in pixels.

parameters.name: cullface
parameters.label: Cull Face
parameters.description: Selects which faces to render.

parameters.name: polygonoffset
parameters.label: Polygon Depth Offset
parameters.description: Turns on the polygon offset feature.

parameters.name: mattr0cols
parameters.label: Mattr0Cols
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.label: Color0Alpha
parameters.description: Help Not Available.
